candidates.name: Playwright MCP / CLI skill
candidates.value: Live dashboard verification, visual regression, accessibility snapshots, and UI workflow checks for agents.
candidates.activation_notes: Enabled with pinned npm package, headless isolated context, localhost origin preference, service workers blocked, image responses omitted, and no unrestricted file access flag.

candidates.name: Semgrep MCP
candidates.value: Agent-triggered generated-code security scans and custom rule validation.
candidates.activation_notes: Enabled with Nix-provided uv, pinned semgrep-mcp package, stdio transport, and no Semgrep cloud token.

candidates.name: GitHub MCP read-only
candidates.blocked_reason: gh auth token is invalid and no Docker, Podman, or github-mcp-server runtime is available on PATH.
candidates.unblock_condition: Provide a scoped GitHub OAuth/PAT path and a pinned local/remote MCP runtime before enabling.
candidates.value: PR, issue, Actions, and code-security context without write access.
candidates.activation_notes: Do not enable write tools until a separate promotion review.

candidates.name: OSV-Scanner
mitigations: Nix-packaged osv-scanner declared in ai-stack system packages
external OSV API use is explicit and visible in candidate permissions
candidate remains pending-rebuild until the binary is on PATH
candidates.unblock_condition: Run the NixOS rebuild, verify osv-scanner is on PATH, then promote state to enabled.
candidates.value: Dependency vulnerability scanning for imported plugins and repo lockfiles.
candidates.activation_notes: Declared through Nix system packages at version 2.2.4; pending rebuild before live use.

candidates.name: Trivy
mitigations: Nix/system-provided trivy binary already on PATH
default command uses filesystem scan only
report output only under .reports/security when output is requested
secret findings must be redacted before sharing or committing reports
vulnerability DB updates are treated as networked maintenance, not implicit import-time execution
candidates.value: Filesystem, secrets, misconfiguration, SBOM, and container/Nix-adjacent security checks.
candidates.activation_notes: Enabled with installed Trivy 0.66.0 and safe default scan args. Cache vulnerability DB outside repo; redact secret values in reports.

candidates.name: Syft and Grype
mitigations: Nix-packaged syft and grype declared in ai-stack system packages
raw local SBOMs are not committed by default
candidate remains pending-rebuild until both binaries are on PATH
candidates.unblock_condition: Run the NixOS rebuild, verify syft and grype are on PATH, then promote state to enabled.
candidates.value: Generate SBOMs and scan them before imported capabilities are promoted.
candidates.activation_notes: Declared through Nix system packages at syft 1.38.0 and grype 0.104.1; pending rebuild before live use. Write reports under .reports/security/ and never commit raw local-only SBOMs by default.

candidates.name: Local MCP admission controller
mitigations: tool security auditor checks every allowlisted tool
regression tests cover enabled candidate policy
candidates.value: Deny-by-default admission and audit reporting before enabling third-party MCP/plugin tools.
candidates.activation_notes: Promote this before enabling any write-capable external tool.

candidates.name: Observability query skill
mitigations: operators must redact sensitive log excerpts before external sharing
candidates.value: Agent-readable health, metrics, journal, and dashboard gap summaries.
candidates.activation_notes: Enabled through aq-report --machine. Redact secrets and surface status in dashboard/aq-report.

candidates.name: NixOS specialist tool pack
mitigations: statix and deadnix are already available on PATH
canonical wrapper runs read-only static checks
non-blocking mode available for exploratory agent use
Nix builds and rebuilds remain separately approval/gate controlled
candidates.value: Nix-specific linting, dependency reasoning, and service hardening analysis.
candidates.activation_notes: Enabled with statix 0.5.8 and deadnix 1.3.1 through the canonical Nix static-analysis wrapper. Use read-only/eval commands by default; gate builds separately.

candidates.name: Code intelligence graph layer
candidates.blocked_reason: Understand-Anything integration is installed, but the repo knowledge graph is still missing.
candidates.unblock_condition: Complete Understand-Anything batch analysis and generate .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json before enabling graph-backed impact tools.
candidates.value: Combine Understand-Anything, lean-ctx, and language-aware indexes for deterministic impact answers.
candidates.activation_notes: Use graph as advisory; verify deploy/security conclusions against source and live checks.

candidates.name: T3MP3ST offensive-security harness
candidates.blocked_reason: Dual-use offensive-security framework with real network, exploit, MCP, and War Room surfaces. Must complete security, license, sandbox, scope, and tool-admission review before cloning, installing, enabling, or exposing tools to agents.
candidates.unblock_condition: Pin an upstream commit, complete multi-agent security and Nix packaging review, enforce scope receipts and human approval gates, add dashboard or aq-report visibility, and pass focused intake plus tier0 validation.
candidates.value: Potentially useful offensive-security orchestration patterns, benchmark verification discipline, scope containment concepts, and MCP/API integration patterns for authorized local harness security testing.
candidates.activation_notes: Do not install or run in this slice. First safe milestone is a review-only quarantine import with no active scanning, no target execution, no secrets, loopback-only services, and all offensive tools denied by default.
